results.name: allocator_default_identity
results.ownership_note: Initial default allocator identity is public allocator behavior relevant to Subset 0.
results.note: Checks whether the initial default allocator is the system default singleton.

results.name: allocator_type_identity
results.ownership_note: Allocator singleton should report the allocator type ID.
results.note: Numeric type IDs may differ across implementations; equality within the process is what matters.

results.name: allocator_singleton_retain_identity
results.ownership_note: Static allocator singletons should retain by pointer identity and release safely.

results.name: allocator_null_allocate
results.note: Expected semantic result is NULL, not a crash.

results.name: allocator_context_roundtrip
results.ownership_note: Allocator context retain/release and get-context roundtrip are public allocator semantics.
results.note: Compares allocator context storage and callback ownership behavior.

results.name: allocator_default_switch
results.ownership_note: Default allocator switching is public behavior relevant to runtime allocation bootstrap.
results.note: On MX this may be thread-scoped; comparison should stay within one thread.

results.name: allocator_reallocate_with_callback
results.ownership_note: Allocator reallocate should use the provided callback when present.
results.note: Tracks callback usage rather than byte-for-byte allocation strategy.

results.name: allocator_reallocate_without_callback
results.ownership_note: Used to detect variance when no explicit reallocate callback exists.
results.note: Observed result pointer was NULL. FX intentionally returns NULL here when no callback is provided.
